# Well Performance Report — Well-A14
**Generated by Reservoir Well Production Analyzer (CrewAI)**
**Field:** Seria Field (SWA) | **Date:** Auto-generated

---

## 1. Well Overview
Well-A14 is a vertical oil producer completed in the Seria Sand Zone 3 at 3,200 ft TVD. The well has been on production for approximately 5 years under primary solution gas drive with no stimulation history. Current conditions indicate a mature production profile with rising GOR and moderate water cut.

---

## 2. Production Performance Summary
- **Current Flow Rate:** 520 STB/day (down from 1,100 STB/day at first production)
- **Cumulative Production:** 0.85 MMSTB
- **Water Cut:** 18% (early-stage water influx)
- **GOR:** 850 scf/STB (above solution GOR — gas cap expansion possible)
- **Drive Mechanism:** Solution gas drive (primary recovery only)

---

## 3. Calculated Metrics

| Metric | Value | Benchmark (Solution Gas Drive) | Assessment |
|---|---|---|---|
| Productivity Index (PI) | 0.800 STB/day/psi | 0.5–2.0 STB/day/psi | Average |
| Recovery Factor (RF) | 20.24% | 15–30% | Good |
| Annual Decline Rate (D) | 14.2%/year | 10–20%/year | Normal |

---

## 4. Key Observations
- **Declining PI trend** is expected with reservoir depletion; no stimulation damage indicated.
- **Recovery factor of ~20%** is consistent with solution gas drive. Significant unswept oil remains if a secondary recovery scheme (waterflooding) is considered.
- **Decline rate of 14%/year** suggests the well has 3–4 years of economic production remaining at current operating costs without intervention.

---

## 5. Recommendations

| Priority | Action | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 1 (High) | **Install Electric Submersible Pump (ESP)** | Reduces FBHP, increases drawdown, boosts PI. Target +150–200 STB/day uplift. |
| 2 (Medium) | **Evaluate Waterflood Feasibility** | RF can increase from ~20% to 35–45% with pressure maintenance. Requires pattern well availability. |
| 3 (Low) | **Perforate Upper Zone** | Seismic shows potential pay in Zone 2. Low-risk workover candidate to offset decline. |
| 4 (Monitor) | **Monitor GOR** | Rising GOR may indicate gas cap breakthrough. Choke management recommended if GOR exceeds 1,200 scf/STB. |
